label: Optimization theory

node.label: Optimization theory
node.definition: Convex and non-convex optimization frame learning as the search for parameters or policies that minimize an objective.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Optimization theory is modeled as depending on Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Linear algebra and feeding downstream nodes such as Dynamical systems & control theory, Game theory & decision theory, Sparse coding & compositional representations.
examples: atlas downstream link to Dynamical systems & control theory
atlas downstream link to Game theory & decision theory
atlas downstream link to Sparse coding & compositional representations
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Calculus & automatic differentiation theory, Linear algebra.

dependencies.rationale: Optimization theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.
Optimization theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
